name: Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba and Pool of Los Chorros
name_original: Presa Romana de Almonacid de la Cuba y Poza de los Chorros
region_intro: Hiking around Almonacid De La Cuba offers diverse landscapes shaped by the Aguasvivas River gully and the Sierra de Belchite. The region features historical landmarks such as the ancient Roman Dam, which is the highest of its kind globally. Trails often follow the river's course, leading to natural swimming spots like Poza de los Chorros. The area also includes hills with historical significance and routes connecting to the Old Village of Belchite. Best hiking trails around Almonacid De La Cuba The most popular hiking route is Almonacid de la Cuba-Belchite Trail, a 4.2 miles (6.7 km) trail that takes 1 hour 47 minutes to complete, connecting the town with the historic village. Another top favourite among local hikers is Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba and Pool of Los Chorros, an easy 3.3 miles (5.3 km) path. This route explores the ancient Roman dam and leads to a popular river spot. Local hikers also love the Poza de los Chorros loop from Belchite, a 7.9 miles (12.8 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes. Hiking around Almonacid De La Cuba is defined by river gully paths, historical sites like the Roman Dam, and the rugged terrain of the Sierra de Belchite. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy riverside walks to moderate loops. The routes in Almonacid De La Cuba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. More than 40 hikers have used komoot to explore Almonacid De La Cuba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: Almonacid De La Cuba features 5 distinct hiking routes, ranging from easy riverside walks to more challenging loops through the Sierra de Belchite.

Q: Are there any easy or family-friendly hiking trails in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes suitable for families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976108152/roman-dam-of-almonacid-de-la-cuba-and-pool-of-los-chorros" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba and Pool of Los Chorros&lt;/a&gt; trail is an easy 3.3-mile path that explores the ancient Roman dam and leads to a popular river spot. Another easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e974295579/almonacid-de-la-cuba-belchite-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Almonacid de la Cuba-Belchite Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which is 4.2 miles long and connects the town with the historic village.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: Many trails in Almonacid De La Cuba are suitable for hiking with dogs, provided they are kept under control, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife. The natural landscapes along the Aguasvivas River and in the Sierra de Belchite offer plenty of opportunities for you and your canine companion to explore.

Q: What historical landmarks can I see while hiking in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: The region is rich in history. You can visit the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5268320" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba&lt;/a&gt;, which is the highest of its kind globally. Many routes also lead to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5247438" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite&lt;/a&gt;, a poignant site with ruins from the Spanish Civil War. Lobo Hill, east of Almonacid de la Cuba, also holds historical significance from the Battle of Belchite.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, there are circular routes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/10418432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de los Chorros loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 7.9-mile trail that takes you through varied terrain, often completed in about 3 hours 26 minutes.

Q: Where can I find natural swimming spots along the trails?
A: The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3592739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Poza de los Chorros&lt;/a&gt; is a popular river spot ideal for swimming on warm days. It's accessible via a well-marked trail from Almonacid and offers stunning views from a dedicated viewpoint.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: The best time to hike in Almonacid De La Cuba is generally during spring and autumn when temperatures are milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summers can be hot, making early mornings or late afternoons preferable, while winters are cooler but still offer pleasant hiking days.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Almonacid De La Cuba?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 7 reviews. Hikers often praise the unique combination of historical sites like the Roman Dam, the scenic beauty of the Aguasvivas River gully, and the diverse terrain of the Sierra de Belchite.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking options?
A: For those seeking a longer adventure, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38150622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Old Village of Belchite – Old Village of Belchite loop from Belchite&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 13.8-mile (22.2 km) route, typically taking over 5 hours to complete.

Q: Are there any other notable natural features to explore?
A: Beyond the Roman Dam and Poza de los Chorros, you can explore the dramatic gully of the Aguasvivas River itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2834036" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Foz de Zafrane&lt;/a&gt;, a spectacular two-kilometer-long ravine near La Puebla de Albortón, also offers impressive natural formations and a large cave.

Q: Is public transport available to reach the hiking trails?
A: While Almonacid De La Cuba is accessible by road, public transport options directly to trailheads might be limited. It's advisable to check local bus schedules if relying on public transport, or consider driving and utilizing available parking near the village center or specific trail starting points.
tips:
Almonacid de la Cuba viewpoint to see one of the most important and best preserved hydraulic works in Roman Hispania.
La Cuba, as this dam is popularly known, is one of the three that regulated the course of the Aguasvivas river, together with the Blesa and Muniesa dams.

Almonacid de la Cuba is a fascinating place to visit!
Roman Dam: The Roman Dam of Almonacid de la Cuba is the highest in the world built during the Roman Empire, at 34 metres high.
Church of Santa María: In the centre of the town is the parish church of Santa María, a construction from the second half of the 16th century with an imposing Mudejar tower.
Almonacid de la Cuba also offers various cultural activities that will allow you to immerse yourself in local life.

Beautiful place to take a dip on hot summer days.
The access (from Belchite) can be somewhat complicated, so basically we will have to look for the Belcars car workshop in Belchite and find a marked wall that will help us start the route on the right foot.
